description_long: This object is a commercial passenger airplane with a streamlined fuselage painted primarily white and a blue vertical stabilizer. The wings are mounted low on the fuselage and extend outward with a slight dihedral angle, each equipped with underwing engines. The airplane features a conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with a row of windows running along the sides, indicating a multi-seat, enclosed configuration. The landing gear is visible beneath the fuselage, with two main gear units under the wings and a nose gear at the front. The overall design is typical of modern short- to medium-haul commercial jets.
description: A white commercial passenger airplane with a blue tail, low-mounted wings, underwing engines, and a forward cockpit.
description_view_0: Front view showing the nose, cockpit windows, and the symmetrical spread of the wings with visible engines beneath.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full fuselage, blue tail fin, wing structure, and underwing engines.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, blue vertical stabilizer, and the trailing edges of the wings and engines.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the fuselage, blue tail, and underwing engines from the other side.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with straight wings and slight dihedral angle, no visible winglets.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main gear units and a single nose gear, fixed.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multi-seat configuration and visible cockpit windows.
